vocab.meaning: người luôn bị ám ảnh bởi thời gian (luôn nhìn đồng hồ)
vocab.example: I don't like wearing a watch because I don't want to feel like a slave to time every single minute.
My smartwatch is on my wrist around the clock to monitor my heart rate even while I sleep.

vocab.meaning: hoàn thành việc gì đó trước thời hạn / chạy đua với thời gian
vocab.example: Having a watch helps me manage my tasks effectively when I'm trying to beat the clock on a deadline.
Some might say wearing a traditional analog watch is a bit behind the times, but I find it quite elegant.

vocab.meaning: từ ngàn đời nay / từ rất lâu rồi
vocab.example: Watches have been seen as a sign of sophistication and class since time immemorial.
I sometimes find myself glancing at my watch repeatedly when I'm trying to kill time in a waiting room.
My watch is always set five minutes fast to ensure I arrive at meetings in the nick of time.

vocab.meaning: thời gian là cốt yếu / vô cùng quan trọng
vocab.example: In my profession, time is of the essence, so wearing a reliable timepiece is absolutely mandatory.

vocab.meaning: hoạt động trơn tru / đều đặn như bộ máy đồng hồ
vocab.example: I appreciate a high-quality mechanical watch where every gear works like clockwork without any glitches.

vocab.word: turn back the hands of time
vocab.example: Looking at this old watch makes me wish I could turn back the hands of time to my childhood.
